chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ أَوْصَى وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – One who bequeaths and upon his is a debt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/41)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/41)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَمُوتُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فَيَضْمَنُهُ ضَامِنٌ لِلْغُرَمَاءِ قَالَ إِذَا رَضِيَ الْغُرَمَاءُ فَقَدْ بَرِئَتْ ذِمَّةُ الْمَيِّتِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who died and upon him was a debt, so a guarantor took the responsibility to the creditors. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If the creditors are happy, so it has freed the deceased from the responsibility’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who dies with debts on him and a guarantor takes responsibility to pay the creditors, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If the creditors agree, the deceased becomes free from responsibility.”’
